Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML
Unit 13: Explainability, Uncertainty, and Robustness

Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz

FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg

01. Title: Explainability, Uncertainty, and Robustness

  • Unit scope and capstone role.

02. Learning outcomes

  • Critically assess trustworthiness of ML systems.

03. Recap from Units 1–12

  • Integrated mathematical foundations.

04. Why trustworthiness matters

  • High-stakes decision implications.

05. Explainability taxonomy

  • Global/local, intrinsic/post-hoc.

06. Interpretation methods overview

  • Feature importance, saliency, counterfactuals.

07. Limits of explainability methods

  • Instability and ambiguity risks.

08. Uncertainty taxonomy

  • Aleatoric vs epistemic decomposition.

09. Predictive uncertainty outputs

  • Intervals, distributions, confidence maps.

10. Calibration fundamentals

  • Reliability vs sharpness.

11. Miscalibration diagnostics

  • ECE, reliability curves.

12. OOD detection basics

  • Shift awareness and novelty signals.

13. Distribution shift taxonomy

  • Covariate, label, concept shift.

14. Robustness definitions

  • Adversarial, statistical, operational.

15. Adversarial perturbation intuition

  • Sensitivity of learned boundaries.

16. Robust optimization overview

  • Min-max perspective.

17. Data augmentation for robustness

  • Distributional support expansion.

18. Regularization and robustness link

  • Capacity control effects.

19. Ensemble and Bayesian robustness

  • Uncertainty and stability gains.

20. Decision-theoretic framing

  • Risk-aware prediction and abstention.

21. Selective prediction

  • Reject option and coverage tradeoff.

22. Cost-sensitive decision thresholds

  • Utility-aligned operating points.

23. Fairness and subgroup reliability (light)

  • Robustness across populations.

24. Causal robustness hints

  • Invariant predictors concept.

25. Stress testing methodology

  • Scenario and perturbation suites.

26. Robustness benchmarks

  • In-distribution vs OOD evaluation.

27. Monitoring post-deployment

  • Drift + calibration surveillance.

28. Human-in-the-loop safeguards

  • Override and audit procedures.

29. Failure mode #1

  • Plausible explanations for wrong predictions.

30. Failure mode #2

  • Overconfident OOD predictions.

31. Failure mode #3

  • Brittle behavior under small shifts.

32. Mitigation checklist

  • Practical trustworthiness workflow.

33. Materials example: robust defect classifier

  • Explainability + uncertainty integration.

34. Materials example: uncertainty-aware optimization

  • Safe candidate selection.

35. Materials example: shift monitoring in production

  • Operational resilience.

36. Governance and accountability

  • Documentation and sign-off models.

37. Reproducibility requirements

  • Seeds, datasets, and audit trails.

38. Communication best practices

  • Transparent reporting of limits.

39. Synthesis of semester concepts

  • From probability to robust deployment.

40. Exam roadmap

  • Integrative concept map.

41. Exercise scaffold: calibration + OOD audit

  • Reliability stress test.

42. Exercise scaffold: explanation stability check

  • Perturbation-based comparison.

43. Exercise scaffold: robustness benchmark

  • Shifted evaluation suite.

44. Exercise scaffold: abstention policy design

  • Risk-threshold implementation.

45. Exercise scaffold: trust report

  • Consolidated model card extension.

46. Exam-oriented key statements

  • High-yield final-unit concepts.

47. Summary table

  • Methods, assumptions, failure modes.

48. Unit summary

  • Core takeaways.

49. References

  • Source list.

50. Course closure slide

  • Final synthesis and outlook.
